violations: Plan follows TDD but phases are mixed stub/impl pattern rather than pure TDD
Some phases don't have clear TDD separation (e.g., phase 3 is stub but also modifies interface)
No explicit mutation testing requirement mentioned in phases
No explicit property-based testing requirement mentioned in phases
Phase numbering inconsistent with complete phase list (gaps in sequence)
Missing verification phases for some implementation phases
LoggingProviderWrapper integration not explicitly verified in integration tests
No reverse testing patterns found but this is actually good (violation description incorrect)
user_access_points: CLI commands using `/provider openai /model gpt-5` or `/model o3`
Save/Load functionality via `/chat save` and `/chat load`
Conversation history automatically maintains response IDs
Integration tests for OpenAI responses tracking
old_code_to_remove: Hardcoded undefined for conversation_id in OpenAIProvider
Hardcoded undefined for previous_response_id in OpenAIProvider
All IMessage type imports throughout codebase

detailed_analysis.integration_strength: STRONG - Plan identifies specific existing files that will use the feature and shows how sessionId flows from GeminiChat through ContentGenerator to providers
detailed_analysis.isolation_risk: LOW - Feature integrates with existing system from the start, updates existing interfaces rather than creating new ones
detailed_analysis.pseudocode_quality: GOOD - Numbered lines, clear algorithmic steps, references requirements, covers all modifications
detailed_analysis.tdd_compliance: PARTIAL - Has test phases but mixes stub/implementation patterns instead of pure test-first approach
detailed_analysis.testing_coverage: ADEQUATE - Behavioral tests defined but missing explicit mutation and property-based testing requirements
detailed_analysis.phase_organization: GOOD - Self-contained phases with clear dependencies and verification steps
detailed_analysis.requirement_mapping: EXCELLENT - All requirements explicitly mapped to phases with pseudocode line references

recommendation: APPROVE WITH CONDITIONS - The plan has strong integration focus and avoids building in isolation. However, it needs to explicitly require mutation testing (80% minimum) and property-based testing (30% minimum) in the verification phases. The TDD pattern could be strengthened by having pure test-first phases rather than mixed stub/impl phases.
